numeraire: ETH wei (18 decimals); stETH valued 1:1 with ETH (protocol sums at parity)
note: Single canonical invariant oracle shared verbatim by (a) the agent runtime asserter (agent/src/oracle.js) and (b) the C4A README Main-Invariants section. Each non-DEG entry is evaluable purely from public chain state (a getter plus, for SOLV-01, one mandated eth_getStorageAt slot-11 raw read). DEG entries are statistical: realized EV/RTP/ROI estimated from the FullTicketResult event stream plus an independent honest-reel re-derivation. Generated from the v73 frozen invariant suite (test/fuzz/invariant/*.inv.t.sol + helpers/SolvencyObligations.sol).

invariants.identity: The game contract's live ETH balance always covers its canonical ETH-obligation set. LIVE (not gameOver): obligations = currentPrizePool + nextPrizePool + futurePrizePool + claimablePool + yieldAccumulator + (freeze-window pending buffer: pendingNext + pendingFuture). POST-game-over: the live pools are dead/zeroed and the only withdrawable obligation is claimablePool, so obligations collapses to claimablePool alone.
invariants.onchainRead: If game.gameOver()==true: obligations = game.claimablePoolView(). Else: game.currentPrizePoolView() + game.nextPrizePoolView() + game.futurePrizePoolView() + game.claimablePoolView() + game.yieldAccumulatorView() + (pendingNext|pendingFuture decoded from eth_getStorageAt(game, slot 11) as next=uint128(word), future=uint128(word>>128)). Compare against eth_getBalance(game). Pool getters at DegenerusGame.sol:2146/2077/2083/2152/2192; gameOver public var DegenerusGameStorage.sol:276; pending buffer has NO external view (slot 11 raw read mandatory).
invariants.comparator: assertGe(eth_getBalance(game), obligations); tolerance = exact (>=, 0 wei slack)

invariants.identity: The summed four-pool obligation (current+next+future+claimable) is always fully backed by the ETH+stETH the game holds — no pool transfer (future->next->current consolidation, the time-based future skim, or a jackpot settlement crediting claimable) can inflate the total above real liquid backing.
invariants.onchainRead: sumPools = game.currentPrizePoolView() + game.nextPrizePoolView() + game.futurePrizePoolView() + game.claimablePoolView(); backing = eth_getBalance(game) + IERC20(STETH_TOKEN).balanceOf(game). STETH_TOKEN at ContractAddresses.sol:61.
invariants.comparator: assertLe(sumPools, backing); tolerance = exact (<=, 0 wei slack)

invariants.identity: Conservation: the summed four-pool obligation can never exceed the real ETH that entered the contract (starting backing captured at session start + cumulative real ETH inflow from buys). An internal transfer only reshapes the split across the four pools; it adds nothing to real inflow, so it can never mint unbacked credit. (Off-chain client tracks startingBacking once at connect and accumulates msg.value of its own successful buys as the realInflow lower bound; SOLV-02 is the always-evaluable on-chain-only form of the same property.)
invariants.onchainRead: sumPools (as SOLV-02) compared to startingBacking + ghost_realInflow, where startingBacking = eth_getBalance(game)+stETH balance sampled at session connect and ghost_realInflow = client-tracked cumulative buy value. On-chain-evaluable surrogate is SOLV-02 (sumPools <= balance+stETH).
invariants.comparator: assertLe(sumPools, startingBacking + realInflow); tolerance = exact (<=)

invariants.identity: Master SOLVENCY-01 identity: claimablePool equals the sum over all addresses of (claimable low-half + afking high-half) of their packed balance slot. The afking reservation rides INSIDE claimablePool; every afking/claimable mutation pairs a claimablePool move, so a dropped paired += or a double-counted half breaks this exactly.
invariants.onchainRead: For each tracked address a: packed = eth_getStorageAt(game, keccak256(abi.encode(a, 7))); sum += uint128(packed) + (packed>>128). Per-address public surrogates: game.claimableWinningsOf(a) (DegenerusGame.sol:2365, returns 0 once swept) and game.afkingFundingOf(a) (DegenerusGame.sol:1408). Aggregate read: game.claimablePoolView() (DegenerusGame.sol:2152). balancesPacked is slot 7 [afking:hi128 | claimable:lo128].
invariants.comparator: assertEq(game.claimablePoolView(), sum-over-addresses(halves)); tolerance = exact (==)

invariants.identity: claimablePool never exceeds the game's liquid backing (ETH + stETH) — the claim liability is always covered after any afking-funded buy, packed credit/debit, stale cashout, or smite.
invariants.onchainRead: game.claimablePoolView() compared to eth_getBalance(game) + IERC20(STETH_TOKEN).balanceOf(game).
invariants.comparator: assertLe(game.claimablePoolView(), eth_getBalance(game)+stETH); tolerance = exact (<=)

invariants.identity: Segregated redemption ETH on sDGNRS never exceeds what the sDGNRS contract can cover in ETH+stETH — redemption obligations stay solvent and segregated from game pools.
invariants.onchainRead: sdgnrs.pendingRedemptionEthValue() (sDGNRS.sol:530) compared to eth_getBalance(sdgnrs) + IERC20(STETH_TOKEN).balanceOf(sdgnrs).
invariants.comparator: assertGe(ethBal+stethBal, sdgnrs.pendingRedemptionEthValue()); tolerance = exact (>=)

invariants.identity: A redemption claim is deleted before payout; a second claim for the same (beneficiary, period) reverts. No double-claim ever succeeds.
invariants.onchainRead: Behavioral: off-chain client issues claimRedemption for a resolved (beneficiary, period), then re-issues the same claim and asserts the second reverts. Public state probe: per-claim slot pendingRedemptions[beneficiary][period] reads zero/empty after the first successful claim (sDGNRS.sol:1128).
invariants.comparator: second-claim must revert; ghost_doubleClaim == 0; tolerance = exact (count == 0)

invariants.identity: The redemption period index is monotonically non-decreasing across observations (it is day-keyed: currentPeriod = GameTimeLib.currentDayIndex()), so it never regresses.
invariants.onchainRead: Client samples the resolving/pending day index over time. Day progression is observable via game.level() (public, DegenerusGameStorage.sol:236) and block.timestamp-derived day; resolved periods surface via the RedemptionResolved(periodIndex, roll) event (sDGNRS.sol:197). Assert each new sample >= previous.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(ghost_periodIndexDecreased, 0) i.e. successive samples non-decreasing; tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: sDGNRS totalSupply equals initialSupply + cumulative mints - cumulative burns (no unbacked share mint or lost burn).
invariants.onchainRead: sdgnrs.totalSupply() (sDGNRS.sol:524) compared to client-tracked initialSupply + totalMinted - totalBurned (client samples totalSupply at connect as initial, accumulates observed Transfer mints/burns).
invariants.comparator: assertEq(sdgnrs.totalSupply(), initialSupply + minted - burned); tolerance = exact (==)

invariants.identity: Within a redemption period, cumulative burned never exceeds half the period's supply snapshot (50% cap enforced).
invariants.onchainRead: snapshot = eth_getStorageAt(sdgnrs, slot 13); burned = eth_getStorageAt(sdgnrs, slot 15) (these fields have no public getter). If snapshot>0, assert burned <= snapshot/2. Behavioral surrogate: a burn() that would exceed half the snapshot reverts.
invariants.comparator: assertLe(periodBurned, supplySnapshot/2); tolerance = exact (<=)

invariants.identity: Every resolved redemption period's roll lies in [25, 175].
invariants.onchainRead: Parse RedemptionResolved(uint24 periodIndex, uint16 roll) events (sDGNRS.sol:197) and assert 25 <= roll <= 175 for each.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(ghost_rollOutOfBounds, 0) i.e. every observed roll in [25,175]; tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: For every redemption claim, ethDirect + lootboxEth == totalRolledEth (the rolled ETH is conserved across the direct/lootbox split).
invariants.onchainRead: Parse RedemptionClaimed events emitted by sDGNRS on claim; assert per-claim ethDirect + lootboxEth == totalRolledEth (and the running cumulative sums stay equal).
invariants.comparator: assertEq(totalEthDirect+totalLootboxEth, totalRolledEth); tolerance = exact (==)

invariants.identity: FLIP accounting identity: totalSupply + vaultMintAllowance == supplyIncUncirculated at all times (no token minted outside the tracked supply struct).
invariants.onchainRead: coin.totalSupply() (FLIP.sol:274) + coin.vaultMintAllowance() (FLIP.sol:288) compared to coin.supplyIncUncirculated() (FLIP.sol:281).
invariants.comparator: assertEq(totalSupply+vaultMintAllowance, supplyIncUncirculated); tolerance = exact (==)

invariants.identity: The vault's FLIP mint allowance never exceeds supplyIncUncirculated (the allowance is a subset of the uncirculated total; it cannot mint beyond the bookkept ceiling).
invariants.onchainRead: coin.vaultMintAllowance() (FLIP.sol:288) compared to coin.supplyIncUncirculated() (FLIP.sol:281).
invariants.comparator: assertLe(coin.vaultMintAllowance(), coin.supplyIncUncirculated()); tolerance = exact (<=)

invariants.identity: The FLIP supply identity (COIN-01) still holds after vault burn/escrow operations (burnCoin/burnEth/vaultEscrow do not corrupt totalSupply + vaultMintAllowance == supplyIncUncirculated).
invariants.onchainRead: Same reads as COIN-01 (coin.totalSupply() + coin.vaultMintAllowance() vs coin.supplyIncUncirculated()), re-evaluated after any vault-touching action.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(totalSupply+vaultMintAllowance, supplyIncUncirculated); tolerance = exact (==)

invariants.identity: The game level is monotonically non-decreasing — it only ever increases (advanceGame increments; no path decrements).
invariants.onchainRead: game.level() (public state var, DegenerusGameStorage.sol:236). Client samples successive level() reads and asserts each >= the previous max.
invariants.comparator: successive game.level() samples non-decreasing (ghost_levelDecreaseCount==0); tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: gameOver is a one-way latch: once it reads true it never reverts to false (terminality of the end state).
invariants.onchainRead: game.gameOver() (public bool, DegenerusGameStorage.sol:276). Client samples over time; once observed true, every later read must remain true.
invariants.comparator: no true->false transition (ghost_gameOverRevival==0); tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: Liveness / no-brick: while the game is live (not gameOver) the core external surface (purchase, advanceGame, placeDegeneretteBet/resolve, claimWinnings) must not revert for a well-formed call due to an internal state corruption — every reachable action either succeeds or reverts only on a documented guard, never on a permanent dead-state. Every Degenerette resolve in particular must succeed (no revert/brick) for any ticket/hero/seed.
invariants.onchainRead: Behavioral: client drives the external action surface and observes success/expected-revert; reads game.gameOver(), game.jackpotPhase() (DegenerusGame.sol:2247), game.level() to classify the FSM state (PURCHASE = !jackpotPhase && !gameOver; JACKPOT = jackpotPhase && !gameOver; GAMEOVER = gameOver) and confirm exactly one is active. A resolveDegeneretteBets that reverts on a structurally valid bet is a brick signal.
invariants.comparator: every well-formed live action succeeds or reverts only on an allowlisted guard; resolve never reverts; tolerance = exact (no unexpected revert)

invariants.identity: Ticket-owed tracking is consistent: an address that never purchased has zero ticketsOwed at every level, and no player carries corrupted/negative owed state.
invariants.onchainRead: game.ticketsOwedView(level, player) (DegenerusGame.sol:2091). For a never-participating address (e.g. 0xDEAD) assert ticketsOwedView(currentLevel, a)==0 and ticketsOwedView(currentLevel+1, a)==0; for tracked actors assert returned values are sane (no overflow).
invariants.comparator: assertEq(game.ticketsOwedView(lvl, nonParticipant), 0); tolerance = exact (==0)

invariants.identity: Every persisted (not-yet-opened) lootbox/presale box record (base != 0) for an active index is present in the permissionless openBoxes() auto-open queue (boxPlayers[index]) until opened — a box owner cannot hold a persisted box un-enqueued and time its open to a favorable level/boon (the WHALE-01 property).
invariants.onchainRead: Internal maps lootboxEth[index][who], presaleBoxEth[index][who], boxPlayers[index] have no production external view; off-chain the client tracks box-creating actions and the auto-open queue via emitted events (box creation events + openBoxes processing events). For each (index, owner) it created with a persisted base, assert membership in the auto-open queue is observable before the box is opened. (On a dev rig this maps to the BoxQueueViewer etch reads lootboxAmountFor/presaleBoxBaseFor/boxPlayersContains.)
invariants.comparator: for every (index,owner) with base != 0: enqueued == true; tolerance = exact (boolean)

invariants.identity: While the VRF window is open (rngLocked()==true), no player-controllable action mutates, in isolation, any storage slot the pending RNG consumption reads — the enumerated in-window read set is frozen: (1) rngWordByDay[currentDay], (2) lootboxRngWordByIndex[index], (3) the lootboxRngPacked cursor (low 48 bits), (4) dailyIdx. advanceGame (the exempt heartbeat) is never measured against the property.
invariants.onchainRead: Client polls game.rngLocked() (DegenerusGame.sol:2214); when true it snapshots the enumerated slots — game.rngWordForDay(currentDay) (DegenerusGame.sol:2207) for (1), and eth_getStorageAt for the lootbox word (slot 35), the lootboxRngPacked cursor (slot 34 low 48 bits, mask 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF), and dailyIdx (slot 0 byte 3) for (2)-(4). It fires an in-window player action (placement/purchase/openBoxes) with NO advanceGame between snapshot and re-check, then re-reads the same slots and asserts byte-equality.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(slot_after, slot_before) for every enumerated in-window slot (ghost_frozenSlotMutations==0); tolerance = exact byte-equality

invariants.identity: Ordering: _swapAndFreeze cannot advance the lootbox read index while any read-slot ticket remains undrained — captured entropy always equals the populated lootboxRngWordByIndex[X] and is never zero (no drain runs against an unpopulated slot).
invariants.onchainRead: Parse TraitsGenerated emissions and the lootbox word at the consumed index; assert each emit's captured entropy == game.rngWordForDay/lootboxRngWordByIndex at that index (eth_getStorageAt slot 35 keyed by index) and entropy != 0.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(capturedEntropy, lootboxRngWordByIndex[X]) and capturedEntropy != 0 (ghost_bindingMismatches==0 and ghost_zeroEntropyConsumptions==0); tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: lootboxRngIndex never skips a value and never double-increments on a single request, and every unlocked index has a nonzero VRF-derived word (no orphaned index).
invariants.onchainRead: Client tracks the lootbox index cursor across VRF fulfillments (eth_getStorageAt slot 34 low 48 bits, the index field) and reads the word per index (slot 35 keyed by index, or via the lootbox-word read path); asserts the index advances by exactly one per request and every reached index has word != 0.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(ghost_indexSkipViolations,0); assertEq(ghost_doubleIncrementCount,0); assertEq(ghost_orphanedIndices,0); tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: A VRF coordinator swap never flips rngLocked in either direction: a daily request in flight keeps the lock until the re-issued word lands; an idle/mid-day-only state stays unlocked. Gap days are backfilled with nonzero rngWordForDay after recovery.
invariants.onchainRead: game.rngLocked() (DegenerusGame.sol:2214) sampled before/after a coordinator swap must be unchanged; for each gap day d after recovery assert game.rngWordForDay(d) != 0 (DegenerusGame.sol:2207).
invariants.comparator: rngLocked unchanged across swap (ghost_stateViolations==0); assertNe(game.rngWordForDay(gapDay),0) for all gap days (ghost_gapBackfillFailures==0); tolerance = exact

invariants.identity: For every (N goldQuadrants, heroIsGold) sub-case, the honest Degenerette base payout EV over that sub-case's own Variant-2 score distribution is never EV-positive: EV <= 100 centi-x (and ~neutral, >= 99.95). No (N, heroIsGold) configuration yields a positive-expectation honest bet.
invariants.onchainRead: Per resolved honest (FLIP) spin the client reads FullTicketResult(player, betId, ticketIndex, playerTicket, matches, payout) (DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:103/788). It derives the decoded base per score s as base = payout*1e6/(perTicket*roiBps) and accumulates payout/wager across many spins per (N, heroIsGold) bucket to estimate realized EV; the decoded base per spin must never exceed that N's S=9 pin. roiBps is reconstructed from the bet's activity score (the on-chain ROI curve). N = goldCount(playerTicket); heroIsGold from the ticket+hero.
invariants.comparator: realized honest EV <= 100 centi-x (and per-spin decoded base <= S9_PIN[N]); statistical tolerance: EV in [99.95, 100] centi-x over a sufficient sample; exact bound on per-spin base (<=)

invariants.identity: The held-fixed economic pins are unchanged by the Variant-2 diff: the activity ROI curve is 90%->99.9% (ROI_MIN 9000 -> 9990 bps), the WWXRP RTP curve is 70%->115%->118%->120% with a 70% floor, and the per-N S=9 jackpot payout pins are exactly [10756411, 12583037, 14792939, 17512324, 20916435]. P(S=9) is placement-independent and equals the all-8-axes match event. Together these fix the realized WWXRP RTP at the jackpot tier.
invariants.onchainRead: Statistical/source pins. Realized ROI is observable from honest spin payout vs wager (FullTicketResult payout/wager trends to 90-99.9%); realized WWXRP RTP from WWXRP-currency FullTicketResult payout/wager trends to the 70/115/118/120% curve with a 70% floor; the S=9 jackpot payout magnitude per N is read directly off a max-score (matches==9) FullTicketResult.payout and must match the per-N pin after ROI scaling. (Source-byte gates assert the curve constants/pins are byte-identical to the pre-v73 source.)
invariants.comparator: realized ROI within [90%,99.9%]; realized WWXRP RTP tracks 70/115/118/120% floor-70%; S=9 payout magnitude matches per-N pin; statistical tolerance on realized curves (sample-bounded), exact on the pinned constants

invariants.identity: The WWXRP rig only ever LIFTS the score (rigged S in [honestS, honestS+2]) and can NEVER fabricate the S=9 jackpot: a rigged S==9 requires the honest pre-rig reel to already be a full 8-axis match (M==8). The rig forces at most one score-bearing cell when M<=6 (a +1 cell or a +2 color-unlock cell), is a no-op when M>=7, and so a fired roll stays S<=8 — leaving P(S=9) exactly invariant vs the honest reel.
invariants.onchainRead: For a WWXRP-currency spin the client reads FullTicketResult.matches (the rigged score s) (DegenerusGameDegeneretteModule.sol:788). To evaluate the never-fabricate bound it independently derives the honest pre-rig reel from the spin's lootbox rng word + index + QUICK_PLAY_SALT (DegenerusTraitUtils.packedTraitsDegenerette) and computes honestS/honestM against the player ticket and hero quadrant; then asserts honestS <= s <= honestS+2 and, if s==9, honestM==8. P(S=9) parity is checked statistically across WWXRP vs honest-equivalent samples per N.
invariants.comparator: assertGe(s, honestS) && assertLe(s, honestS+2); if s==9 then assertEq(honestM, 8); P(S=9) WWXRP == honest within statistical tolerance (analytical rel-diff < 1e-12); per-spin bounds exact

invariants.identity: The per-player curse counter is well-formed (a uint8 stack count) and curse-only operations (smite / decurse / stale-cashout curse-set) are pool-neutral — they move claimablePool by exactly zero and leave the SOLV-04 half-sum identity byte-unchanged.
invariants.onchainRead: game.curseCountOf(player) (DegenerusGame.sol:2306) read before/after a smite/decurse/stale-cashout; assert the curse count changes as expected (e.g. +2 on smite, 0 after decurse) while game.claimablePoolView() (DegenerusGame.sol:2152) is byte-identical across the operation.
invariants.comparator: assertEq(claimablePoolView_after, claimablePoolView_before) for curse-only ops (pool-neutral, exact == 0 delta); curse count within [0,255]
